name: Cuscusu Di Pistachi
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Almond oil (or mild vegetable oil)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the pan for steaming: Mix flour and water in a bowl, then knead briefly to form a sticky dough. Roll into a rope and apply to the rim of the pan. Add water so it's below the bottom of the couscousiere or colander. Bring water to a boil until steam escapes freely, then lower to a simmer.
2. Prepare the couscous: Place grain in a bowl and add water. Rake through with fingers, tilting the bowl to drain excess water. Set aside for 5 minutes.
3. Line the couscousiere with dampened napkin or cheesecloth and add grain. Cover and steam for 15 minutes.
4. Prepare the flavoring: Fill saucepan halfway with water and pistachios. Bring to a boil, then strain. Rub pistachios through towel to remove skins. Process in food processor until smooth paste forms.
5. Add oil to the paste in small amounts, processing between scrapes. Reserve pistachio paste.
6. After 15 minutes of steaming couscous, transfer it to a roasting pan and spread with a fork. Add saltwater mixture and work into cooled couscous using one hand. Fold in pistachio paste three times.
7. Return water to simmer in bottom pan. Line the couscousiere again with cheesecloth and add seasoned couscous. Cover and steam for 15 minutes.
8. Transfer steamed couscous to roasting pan, spread without compressing, and leave uncovered until cool.
9. For finishing: Bring sugar and water to a boil, stirring until all sugar dissolves. Cool syrup. Fluff couscous with palms of hands and toss in the cooled syrup in five additions. Allow couscous to dry uncovered for several hours, fluffing occasionally, until grains separate well.
10. Refrigerate tightly covered up to several days. To serve, fluff couscous with fork, mound on platter, sprinkle grated chocolate, confectioners' sugar over the top, and garnish with candied fruit pieces.
